projects.name: 1991 Mazda Miata

textSections.title: Overview
textSections.text: With the goal of learning the basics of 3D modeling, projection designing and assembly mates, I undertook the task of modeling my favorite car, the 1991 MX-5 in SolidWorks. Using reference pictures, I modeled each static panel of the chassis. I then created separate components for moving parts such as the swing doors, spinning wheels, the hydraulic trunk and the iconic winking headlights. While not an engineering-precise drawing, the model is complete enough to be exported and rendered, opening the door to creative applications such as advertisement visuals, producing 3D printed miniatures and more.

textSections.title: Process
textSections.text:
I began by projecting to-scale reference images of the front, side and top views onto their corresponding axes in SolidWorks. Using the 3D Sketch feature, I was then able to draw freely in space rather than being confined to a single plane. By locking the camera to one reference view at a time, I sketched each body panel along that projection, then switched to another view to adjust and vary the curvature. This created a 3D boundary that curved and bent in space according to all three references simultaneously.

	To fill these boundaries into actual surfaces, I used Surface Loft and Fill Surface, then applied Thicken to give each panel its physical form. This process was repeated for each non-moving body panel on one half of the car, which was then mirrored to complete the symmetry. Separate components such as the wheels, steering wheel, trunk, seats and headlights, were modeled independently using extrusions built up from basic shapes and brought together in the final assembly.

mediaSections.title: To scale reference pictures projected on each axis

projects.shortDescription: A 3D assembly of a 1991 Mazda Miata modeled in SolidWorks

projects.name: ScrySheets

textSections.title: Overview
textSections.text: ScrySheets is an Excel-based deck building and card management tool for Magic: The Gathering. Built using VBA, Power Query and the Scryfall API, it automatically imports card data into a searchable database and streamlines the process of creating, organizing and pricing decks. The project was developed to explore how spreadsheet software can be extended into a functional application through automation and custom user interfaces.

textSections.title: Process
textSections.text: The project began with a Power Query pipeline that retrieves and refreshes card data directly from the Scryfall API. VBA macros were then developed to handle card searching, deck construction, printing and database management. To improve usability, I designed a custom deck editor that automatically categorizes cards by type and tracks deck value. Through this project, I gained experience working with APIs, data transformation, VBA programming and user-focused interface design within Excel.

projects.shortDescription: Card searcher and database built in Excel

sections.title: Who I Am
sections.text:
I'm a Mechanical Engineering student at Concordia University, expected to graduate in 2029. My background spans engineering, IT and web development, giving me a unique mix of technical and problem-solving experience. From building computers and developing websites to creating CAD models and prototypes, I've always enjoyed turning ideas into practical solutions.

	Before pursuing engineering, I worked in IT, earned my CompTIA A+ certification and supported networking, POS and security systems for businesses across Vancouver. Through hands-on projects and industry experience, I discovered a passion for design and engineering that ultimately brought me to Montreal. Today, I'm focused on developing my skills, gaining industry experience and continuing to learn wherever I can.

sections.title: Skills & Experience
sections.text:
A growing foundation in mechanical engineering supported by experience in CAD modeling, prototyping and technical problem solving. My background in IT and web development allows me to work comfortably across both hardware and software environments.

	I bring strong communication skills, experience working with clients and teams, and fluency in English, French and Vietnamese.

Engineering: SolidWorks
Programming: C, C++, JavaScript, HTML, CSS
Development: Frontend & Backend, Git, MySQL
Productivity: Microsoft 365, Word, Excel, PowerPoint
Collaboration: Microsoft Teams

sections.title: Looking Ahead
sections.text: I'm seeking opportunities to gain hands-on engineering experience, learn from industry professionals and contribute to meaningful projects. Outside of school, you'll usually find me training for triathlons, playing badminton or enjoying a board game with friends. If you'd like to connect, feel free to reach out.
